work_unit: SZ-PP-M-006: add timeout to all chrome.runtime.sendMessage calls in popup
situation: All 3 chrome.runtime.sendMessage calls in popup/SettingsPage.tsx (getSettings, saveSettings, testVisionConnection) had no timeout. If background script is unresponsive or disconnected, popup hangs indefinitely — loading spinner never clears, test button stays disabled forever, debounce flush blocks unmount.
action: Added sendMessageWithTimeout<T>(message, timeoutMs) helper that wraps chrome.runtime.sendMessage in a Promise.race with setTimeout. Applied: getSettings=5s, saveSettings=5s (both debounced and flush-on-unmount paths), testVisionConnection=15s (Vision API can be slow). Also wrapped testVisionConnection in try/catch/finally so testing state always resets even on timeout/error.
outcome_evidence: Commit f1d639a on auto/opt-popup-1783215325. 1 file changed, 32 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-). Pushed to origin. cp-guard triggered (SettingsPage.tsx diverged from main due to prior unmerged SZ-PP fixes), used case-104 direct wt commit escape hatch.
next_suggestion: SZ-SH-M-001: No storage quota handling for HistoryItem thumbnails (medium)
SZ-SH-M-002: Source manifest lacks CSP; build.mjs adds wasm-unsafe-eval (medium)

audit_findings.finding: chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ action: 'getSettings' }) had no timeout — popup loading state permanent if background unresponsive
chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ action: 'saveSettings' }) in debounce callback had no timeout
Flush-on-unmount saveSettings also had no timeout
testVisionConnection await with no timeout — button disabled forever on hang; also no error handling (setTesting(false) only on success path)
